name: Raya Real Trail
name_original: Parque Natural Doñana – circular desde Charco de la Boca, El Rocío
description: Hike the 7.7-mile Raya Real Trail in Doñana Natural Park, a moderate route through pine forests and past Charco de la Boca.
region_intro: Family friendly hiking trails in Parc National De Doñana traverse a diverse landscape characterized by extensive marshlands, dynamic dune systems, and Mediterranean forests. The region features 38 kilometers of unspoiled beaches, with some dunes reaching over 30 meters high. Inland, the terrain transitions into coniferous and oak forests, alongside scrubland with stone pines and cork trees. These varied ecosystems provide a range of environments for hiking, from flat wetland paths to sandy coastal routes. Best family friendly hiking trails in Parc National De Doñana The most popular family friendly hiking route is Doñana Natural Park - circular via the Charco de la Boca Trail, a 2.3 miles (3.7 km) trail that takes about 57 minutes to complete. This easy route offers views of the park's significant wetlands and is suitable for families. Another top favourite among local hikers is Matalascañas Dune Park Loop, an easy 3.0 miles (4.9 km) path. This trail explores the unique coastal dune systems, providing an experience of the shifting sands and natural features. Local hikers also love the Marshland Promenade Loop, a 2.7 miles (4.4 km) trail leading through marshland areas, often completed in about 1 hour 6 minutes. This route is ideal for observing the park's birdlife. Hiking in Parc National De Doñana is defined by marshlands, dynamic dunes, and Mediterranean forests. The network offers options for various ability levels, with many easy routes suitable for families. The routes in Parc National De Doñana are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from more than 200 reviews. More than 1500 hikers have used komoot to explore Parc National De Doñana's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many family-friendly hiking trails are there in Parc National De Doñana?
A: Parc National De Doñana offers a wide selection of family-friendly hiking trails. There are over 50 easy routes specifically suitable for families, out of a total of more than 80 trails in the region.

Q: What makes the hiking trails in Parc National De Doñana particularly suitable for families?
A: The trails in Parc National De Doñana are ideal for families due to their generally flat terrain and diverse, engaging landscapes. You can explore everything from extensive marshlands and dynamic dunes to serene pine forests, offering varied scenery that keeps children interested. Many trails are well-maintained and accessible, making them suitable for different age groups.

Q: What kind of wildlife can families expect to see on a hike?
A: Parc National De Doñana is a haven for wildlife. Families often spot a vast array of bird species, including flamingos, especially in the marshlands. Inland, you might encounter red and fallow deer, wild boar, and various reptiles. Keep an eye out for rare species like the Iberian lynx and the Spanish imperial eagle, though sightings of these are less common.

Q: Are there circular routes suitable for families?
A: Yes, there are several excellent circular routes perfect for families. A great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983597795/donana-natural-park-circular-via-the-charco-de-la-boca-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Doñana Natural Park - circular via the Charco de la Boca Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which is easy and allows you to experience the park's natural beauty without retracing your steps. Another good choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e984042109/matalascanas-dune-park-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Matalascañas Dune Park Loop&lt;/a&gt;, offering unique coastal scenery.

Q: What interesting natural features or landmarks can families explore along the trails?
A: Families can discover a variety of natural features and landmarks. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4091378" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Charco de la Boca Trail&lt;/a&gt; itself is a highlight, leading to the tranquil &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5301283" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Charco de la Boca&lt;/a&gt; lake. You can also visit the stunning &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4491807" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torre Carbonero Beach&lt;/a&gt; or the expansive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4557220" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castilla Beach&lt;/a&gt;, both offering beautiful coastal views and dune systems.

Q: Are there stroller-friendly options for families with very young children?
A: While many trails are generally flat, some paths might be sandy or uneven. For very young children in strollers, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983601572/marshland-promenade-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Marshland Promenade Loop&lt;/a&gt; offer more accessible sections, often featuring boardwalks or well-packed surfaces. It's always a good idea to check specific trail conditions if you plan to use a stroller.

Q: What is the best time to visit Parc National De Doñana for family hikes?
A: The best time for family hikes in Parc National De Doñana is typically during spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). During these seasons, the weather is milder and more pleasant for walking, and you'll have excellent opportunities for birdwatching as migratory species pass through. Summers can be very hot, and some areas might be less accessible.

Q: Where can we find visitor information or facilities in the park?
A: Parc National De Doñana has several visitor centers that serve as excellent starting points. These include El Acebuche, La Rocina, and José Antonio Valverde. They provide valuable information about the park, its ecosystems, and available trails, and often have facilities like restrooms and picnic areas.

Q: Is parking available near the family-friendly trails?
A: Yes, parking is generally available at or near the main visitor centers and trailheads for the more popular family-friendly routes. For example, trails originating from visitor centers like El Acebuche usually have designated parking areas, making access convenient for families.

Q: Are dogs allowed on family hiking trails in Parc National De Doñana?
A: Due to its status as a protected national park and UNESCO World Heritage site, strict regulations are in place to protect the delicate ecosystems and wildlife. Dogs are generally not permitted on the trails within the core areas of Parc National De Doñana, even on a leash. It's best to check with a visitor center for specific rules regarding pets in designated areas.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about family-friendly trails in Parc National De Doñana?
A: The komoot community highly rates the trails in Parc National De Doñana, with an average score of 4.2 stars. Reviewers frequently praise the unique biodiversity, the chance to see diverse ecosystems in close proximity, and the well-maintained paths that make for enjoyable family outings. The opportunity for wildlife spotting, especially birds, is also a common highlight.

We collected two large bags that we happened to have with us full of trash, from condoms to diapers, car parts and so on. Shame on you!!!

Located within the Doñana Natural Park and next to the picturesque town of El Rocío, this wonderful natural ecosystem is the ideal place for those who enjoy bird watching and this type of biosphere. It can be traveled along the Paseo Marismeño path, an entertaining walk for the whole family.

faqs.question: Where does the Raya Real Trail start and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The Raya Real Trail is a circular route that begins and ends near Charco de la Boca in El Rocío. You'll find parking options available in the vicinity of the trailhead, making it convenient to access.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The best time to hike this trail, located within Doñana Natural Park, is typically during the cooler months of autumn, winter, and spring. Summers can be very hot, making the hike less enjoyable. Spring offers pleasant temperatures and the chance to see more wildlife and flora.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for the Doñana Natural Park?
faqs.answer: While there isn't a general entrance fee for simply hiking this specific trail within Doñana Natural Park, certain areas or activities within the park might require permits or guided tours. It's always a good idea to check the official Doñana Natural Park website for the latest regulations regarding access and any specific zones you might wish to explore beyond the main trail.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for families with children?
faqs.answer: With a moderate difficulty rating and relatively flat terrain (only about 18 meters of elevation gain), this 12.46 km circular route is generally suitable for families. The duration of approximately 3 hours and 8 minutes means it's a manageable day hike for active children. Remember to bring plenty of water and snacks.

faqs.question: Is the Raya Real Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Given its location within Doñana Natural Park, regulations regarding dogs can be strict to protect wildlife. It's advisable to check the official park rules before bringing your dog. If allowed, dogs should always be kept on a leash to ensure the safety of both your pet and the local fauna.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the route?
faqs.answer: The trail primarily traverses the natural landscapes of Doñana Natural Park. You can expect a mix of paths through the Pine Forest of Doñana Natural Park and potentially sandy or earthy tracks typical of the region. The elevation changes are minimal, making for a generally flat and accessible walk.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: Along the route, you'll experience the serene beauty of Doñana Natural Park. Key highlights include the tranquil Charco de la Boca, a picturesque lake, and sections through the lush Pine Forest of Doñana Natural Park. Keep an eye out for local birdlife and the unique flora of the region.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several other paths. You will find yourself on parts of the 'Raya Real' trail itself, as well as 'Paseo Marismeño', 'Puente del Ajolí', and 'Puente del Ajoli' during your hike.

faqs.question: What should I pack for a hike on this trail?
faqs.answer: For this moderate hike, essential items include comfortable hiking shoes, plenty of water, sun protection (hat, sunscreen, sunglasses), and snacks. Depending on the season, a light jacket might be useful. Binoculars could enhance your wildlife viewing experience.

faqs.question: Are there any public transport options to reach the trailhead?
faqs.answer: Public transport options directly to the trailhead at Charco de la Boca in El Rocío might be limited. It's generally recommended to check local bus schedules or consider driving, as El Rocío is a well-known pilgrimage village with road access. Taxis or ride-sharing services from nearby towns could also be an option.

faqs.question: Is the trail a loop or out-and-back?
faqs.answer: The Raya Real Trail is a circular route, meaning it starts and ends at the same point near Charco de la Boca, allowing you to experience varied scenery without retracing your steps.

waypoints.name: Pine Forest of Doñana Natural Park
